core.label: Verilog Fundamentals
core.description: Hardware description with modules, wires, and registers.

topics.label: Setup & Simulation
topics.description: Install Icarus Verilog or use Vivado. Verilog describes digital circuits.

topics.label: Modules & Ports
topics.description: module with input, output, inout ports. Modules are Verilog's building blocks.

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: wire (combinational), reg (sequential), integer, parameter. wire vs reg is key.

topics.label: Combinational Logic
topics.description: assign for continuous assignment, always @(*) for combinational blocks.

topics.label: Sequential Logic
topics.description: always @(posedge clk) for flip-flops. Non-blocking (<=) vs blocking (=) assignment.

topics.label: Hierarchy & Instantiation
topics.description: Instantiate modules with port connections. Build complex designs from simple modules.

topics.label: Testbenches
topics.description: initial blocks, #delay, $display, $monitor, $finish. Verify designs with simulation.

topics.label: State Machines
topics.description: FSM design with case statements and state encoding.
